scenario_name: Atypical ACS in Elderly Diabetic Patient

data.text: Hello, Mrs. Doe. I’m Dr. Lee. What brings you in to see us today?
Oh, doctor... my stomach has been really bothering me today. Right here in the upper part, just below my ribs.
I’m sorry to hear that. When did this start?
About three or four hours ago, right after breakfast. I took some Tums but it hasn’t helped at all.
I see. Can you describe the pain for me? Is it sharp, burning, or more of an ache?
It’s not really sharp... it's more like a heavy, dull ache. Like someone is pressing down hard on my upper stomach.
Does the pain move anywhere else, like to your back, your neck, or your arms?
Maybe a little bit into my left shoulder, but honestly, it’s mostly just my stomach.
Are you having any other symptoms? Any nausea, vomiting, or feeling short of breath?
I do feel pretty nauseous, yes. And I’ve been feeling kind of clammy and breaking out in a cold sweat. I also felt a little winded just walking.
Mrs. Doe, because of your medical history with diabetes, and this heavy ache with cold sweats, we need to be very careful. Heart issues can disguise themselves as stomach pain in women.
I’m going to order an EKG right now and check some blood work, including a heart test called Troponin, just to make sure everything is okay.
